message:
refactor(sync): fix clippy pedantic issues in tombstone-preservation / VACUUM-INTO code

Fresh-eyes pass after the VACUUM-INTO and tombstone-semantics commits.
`cargo clippy --release` on the pre-series baseline (c4f1165) reported
5 pedantic errors; my series had pushed it to 5 (same count but worse
numbers — `too_many_lines` rose from 175 to 183 after Round 15 added
tombstone-preservation logic inline in
`open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy`, and a new
`unnecessary_wraps` surfaced because
`snapshot_tombstones` moved from a private CLI-level helper into
`pub(crate) sync::snapshot_tombstones` where the rust-analyzer / clippy
pass-through noticed its `Result` wrapper is never used in anger).

This commit brings the clippy error count down to 3 (all of which
predate this series and were already failing CI), with a strictly
smaller "surface" than before:

1) `snapshot_tombstones` loses its `Result` wrapper. Every path in the
   body already logs + returns an empty vec on failure, so the caller
   never observes an `Err`. Callers that used to `.unwrap()` or `?`
   now just take the returned `Vec` directly:
   - `cli::commands::sync::execute` delegation path
   - `cli::commands::sync::execute_import` force/rebuild path
   - `cli::commands::doctor::preserved_tombstones_for_doctor_rebuild`
   - `config::preserved_unflushed_tombstones`
   - 6 unit tests in `cli::commands::sync::tests`

2) `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy` — the inline Round-15
   tombstone-preservation blocks on the `Ok(Some(anomaly))` and
   `Err(probe_err)` arms are now extracted to
   `rebuild_or_defer_after_recoverable_anomaly` and
   `rebuild_or_defer_after_probe_error`, both of which share a
   `rebuild_with_tombstone_preservation` inner helper so the
   "snapshot → drop → rebuild → restore" sequence only exists in one
   place. This drops the main function from 183 lines to 137 and brings
   its stack-frame estimate well under the clippy threshold.

3) `tombstones_missing_from_jsonl_tombstones` now uses `.is_none_or(…)`
   instead of `.map_or(true, …)` per clippy's new lint (Rust 1.82).

4) `compact_database_via_vacuum_into_in_place` docstring reformatted
   so the failure-mode list is a proper paragraph-separated bullet
   list rather than a continuation clippy flagged as
   `doc_lazy_continuation`.

5) The `probe_err: BeadsError` argument on
   `rebuild_or_defer_after_probe_error` was flagged by
   `needless_pass_by_value` (we only use it for display). Taken by
   `&BeadsError` instead.

Remaining clippy errors are all pre-series and not introduced by this
work:
- `too_many_lines` on `sync::execute` (139 vs pre-series 138, +1).
- `too_many_lines` on `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy`
  (137 vs pre-series 175, **−38** thanks to the extraction above).
- `needless_pass_by_value` on `SqliteStorage::get_issue_ids_by_status`
  (pre-existing, touches a public `Status` parameter).

No behavioral change. Suite state: lib 1240, e2e_errors 199,
e2e_sync_artifacts 147, e2e_sync_failure_injection 151,
e2e_workspace_commands 172, repro_3way_merge_bug 138 (2047 total,
all green).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
